reasoning: Currently, the top 7 largest companies by market cap on CompaniesMarketCap are all tech companies (Nvidia, Apple, Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, TSMC, and Broadcom). The first non-tech company is Saudi Aramco at #8, with a market cap of $1.753T. Amazon (#5) has a market cap of $2.563T, giving an $810 billion buffer. Even if a top 5 tech company significantly drops, TSMC and Broadcom are poised to take its place, keeping the top 5 completely tech-oriented. A YES resolution would only be threatened by a massive, concentrated tech crash combined with a sharp surge in oil (Saudi Aramco) or another non-tech sector. Given the 9-month horizon to December 2025, the likelihood of this drastic shift happening is quite low, aligning with the strong community forecast of ~90%.
